title: Iria consanguinea
intro: Iria consanguinea, also known as Wild Fumitory or Consanguineous Fumitory, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the family Papaveraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Iria consanguinea include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Iria consanguinea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, particularly those related to the respiratory and gastrointestinal systems.
taxonomy: Iria consanguinea belongs to the family Asteraceae. It is a type of flowering plant native to Australia. The classification of Iria consanguinea is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, and clade - Asterids. It is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae within the family Asteraceae. Further research may be needed for a complete classification.
